name: Cave of Trophonius
description: The Cave of Trophonius, located near Lebadea in Boeotia, Greece, was a sacred oracle dedicated to the hero Trophonius, son of Apollo (or Erginus, depending on the account). It was renowned for its profoundly unsettling and often traumatic oracular consultations. To seek advice from Trophonius, individuals underwent a rigorous purification process involving several days of ritual cleansing, sacrifices to various deities, and fasting. The supplicant would then descend into the cave through a narrow opening, experiencing a terrifying ordeal in the dark depths. Accounts describe being seized by unseen forces, witnessing strange visions, and undergoing psychological torment. Upon returning to the surface, often in a state of shock and disorientation, the priests of Trophonius would interpret the supplicant's experience and provide the oracle's answer. The experience was so terrifying that it became proverbial: individuals who appeared gloomy or serious were often asked if they had consulted the oracle of Trophonius. The cave represented a journey into the subconscious, a confrontation with one's deepest fears, and a transformative (though often harrowing) encounter with the divine. The oracle was consulted on matters of state, personal affairs, and the future, and its pronouncements were highly regarded, despite the terrifying nature of the experience.
subtitle: Oracle of terror and transformation.

keyMyths.title: The Descent into the Cave
keyMyths.description: Supplicants underwent a complex purification process before entering the cave. The experience inside was terrifying, involving unseen forces and psychological torment, leading to prophetic visions.

keyMyths.title: The Proverbial Gloom
keyMyths.description: The experience at the Cave of Trophonius was so frightening that people who appeared unusually serious or gloomy were often asked if they had consulted the oracle there, highlighting its reputation for causing distress.

sources.author: Pausanias
sources.description: Provides a detailed account of the oracle, its rituals, and the experience of consulting Trophonius.
sources.text: Provides a detailed account of the oracle, its rituals, and the experience of consulting Trophonius.

sources.author: Cicero
sources.description: Mentions the Cave of Trophonius in the context of philosophical discussions about fear and the supernatural.
